subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Waiting for the Rain
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Rakesh was a young boy who lived with his grandfather in Mussoorie in the Himalayan foothills. His parents lived in a village where there were no schools. Therefore, they sent Rakesh to live with his grandfather so that he could attend school and receive a good education.
questions: Who was Rakesh?
Where did Rakesh live with his grandfather?
Why did his parents send him there?
What was special about Mussoorie?
What was the purpose of Rakesh living with his grandfather?

passages.paragraph: One day Rakesh bought some cherries from the Mussoorie bazaar. After eating the fruit, he kept the seed with him. His grandfather suggested that instead of keeping the seed, he should plant it in the garden. Rakesh followed the advice and planted the seed in the soil.
questions: What did Rakesh buy from the bazaar?
What did he do after eating the cherries?
What advice did his grandfather give?
Where did Rakesh plant the seed?
Why did Rakesh decide to plant the seed?

passages.paragraph: After some days, Rakesh noticed a small twig growing in the garden. He realized that the cherry seed had begun to grow into a plant. Rakesh felt happy and began to take care of the plant by watering it regularly.
questions: What did Rakesh notice in the garden?
What did the twig represent?
How did Rakesh feel about the plant?
What did he do to care for the plant?
Why was the twig important?

passages.paragraph: One day a goat entered the garden and ate the leaves of the young cherry plant. Only the thin stem remained. Rakesh felt very sad because he thought the plant might not survive.
questions: What animal entered the garden?
What did the goat do to the plant?
What part of the plant remained?
How did Rakesh feel?
Why was he worried about the plant?

passages.paragraph: Despite the damage caused by the goat, the cherry plant slowly began to grow again. New shoots appeared and the plant became stronger. Rakesh continued to take care of it with patience and attention.
questions: Did the plant stop growing after the damage?
What appeared on the plant again?
How did the plant change over time?
How did Rakesh care for the plant?
What quality did Rakesh show?

passages.paragraph: Later, a woman cutting grass accidentally cut the cherry plant with her scythe. Once again the plant was damaged. However, the strong little tree survived and continued to grow.
questions: Who accidentally cut the plant?
What tool did she use?
What happened to the cherry plant?
Did the plant die?
What does this show about the plant?

passages.paragraph: As the years passed, the cherry tree grew taller and stronger. Rakesh often looked at the tree with pride because he had planted it himself. He felt a special bond with the tree.
questions: What happened to the cherry tree over the years?
How did Rakesh feel about the tree?
Why did he feel proud?
What kind of bond did he feel?
Why was the tree important to him?

passages.paragraph: One day Rakesh noticed a pale pink blossom on the cherry tree. It was the first flower the tree had produced. Rakesh and his grandfather looked at it with wonder and happiness.
questions: What did Rakesh notice on the tree?
What color was the blossom?
Why was the blossom special?
Who admired the blossom?
How did they feel?

passages.paragraph: Soon the tree produced small cherries that attracted birds like bulbuls and scarlet minivets. The birds happily ate the fruit while Rakesh watched them with interest.
questions: What grew on the cherry tree?
Which birds came to the tree?
Why did the birds come?
What did the birds eat?
How did Rakesh react?

passages.paragraph: The story shows that patience, care, and love for nature can create something beautiful. From a tiny seed, a strong cherry tree grew. Rakesh learned that nurturing nature brings joy and satisfaction.
questions: What lesson does the story teach?
What qualities helped the tree grow?
What grew from the tiny seed?
What did Rakesh learn from the experience?
Why is caring for nature important?
